main.py
data.txt

# GIT STATUS
Current branch: HEAD
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Status:
M main.py

Recent commits:
c961c7b chore: automated format fix 3
59262c1 chore: automated lint fix 5
074f929 chore: automated lint fix 4
222d0c2 feat: add process function
76996e3 chore: automated format fix 2

# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

The repository at `/home/user/repo` contains 12 commits interleaved between a human author and `bot@company.com`. Consecutive bot commits should be squashed into single commits with descriptions in the format `chore: [bot] squashed N automated commits`. Human commits must remain untouched. After rewriting, update the `main` bookmark and write a squash log to `/home/user/squash_log.txt` showing which original commits were merged into each squashed commit.
Let me start by examining the repository structure and git log to understand the commit history.
Let me start by examining the repository and its commit history.

parts.text:
Now I have a full picture. Let me map out the commit chain:

```
d8ae9b40  test  feat: add cleanup function        ← main bookmark (HEAD working copy)
c961c7b5  bot   chore: automated format fix 3
59262c1a  bot   chore: automated lint fix 5
074f9292  bot   chore: automated lint fix 4
222d0c21  test  feat: add process function
76996e3b  bot   chore: automated format fix 2
133e651f  bot   chore: automated lint fix 3
6243d64a  test  feat: add main module
f307015e  bot   chore: automated format fix 1
1e775ed7  bot   chore: automated lint fix 2
c48cb254  bot   chore: automated lint fix 1
37362327  test  feat: initial data setup           ← root
```

Bot runs to squash (oldest→newest):
- **Group A**: `c48cb254`, `1e775ed7`, `f307015e` (3 commits) → after `37362327`
- **Group B**: `133e651f`, `76996e3b` (2 commits) → after `6243d64a`
- **Group C**: `074f9292`, `59262c1a`, `c961c7b5` (3 commits) → after `222d0c21`

todos.content: Plan squash groups and verify commit content
Rewrite history: squash bot commit groups using jj
Update main bookmark to new tip
Write squash_log.txt with mapping of original commits

parts.text: Now I'll inspect each commit's tree to understand what content to preserve, then perform the rewrite. Since this is a jj repo, I'll use `jj` operations to rebuild the history.

parts.text:
Now I have everything I need. I'll rewrite the history using `git` (which jj's backing store uses). The strategy:

1. Start from root commit `37362327` (human)
2. Create squash commit A = tree of `f307015e` with parent `37362327`, author `bot@company.com`
3. Cherry-pick `6243d64a` (human) on top
4. Create squash commit B = tree of `76996e3b` with parent new-6243d64a
5. Cherry-pick `222d0c21` (human) on top
6. Create squash commit C = tree of `c961c7b5` with parent new-222d0c21
7. Cherry-pick `d8ae9b40` (human) on top
8. Update `main` bookmark to new tip

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && git cat-file -p 37362327^{tree}

parts.text: I have all tree SHAs. Now I'll build the new history using `git commit-tree` and author overrides for bot commits, then update everything via jj.

input.command:
cd /home/user/repo && \
# Get original author dates for representative commits
DATE_A=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" f307015e) && \
DATE_B=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" 76996e3b) && \
DATE_C=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" c961c7b5) && \
DATE_HUMAN1=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" 6243d64a) && \
DATE_HUMAN2=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" 222d0c21) && \
DATE_HUMAN3=$(git log -1 --format="%ai" d8ae9b40) && \
echo "DATE_A=$DATE_A DATE_B=$DATE_B DATE_C=$DATE_C"

cd /home/user/repo && \
ROOT=37362327a66f8cb9bcbd1034d04f83c4a5009847 && \

# Squash A: 3 bot commits (c48cb254, 1e775ed7, f307015e) after root
# Tree = f307015e tree
TREE_A=$(git rev-parse f307015e^{tree}) && \
SQUASH_A=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Bot User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:03 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Bot User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:03 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_A -p $ROOT -m "chore: [bot] squashed 3 automated commits") && \
echo "SQUASH_A=$SQUASH_A" && \

# Human commit 1: feat: add main module (6243d64a), parent=SQUASH_A
# Need to re-create with same tree, author, message but new parent
TREE_H1=$(git rev-parse 6243d64a^{tree}) && \
H1=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Test User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:04 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Test User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:04 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_H1 -p $SQUASH_A -m "feat: add main module") && \
echo "H1=$H1" && \

# Squash B: 2 bot commits (133e651f, 76996e3b) after H1
TREE_B=$(git rev-parse 76996e3b^{tree}) && \
SQUASH_B=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Bot User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:05 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Bot User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:05 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_B -p $H1 -m "chore: [bot] squashed 2 automated commits") && \
echo "SQUASH_B=$SQUASH_B" && \

# Human commit 2: feat: add process function (222d0c21), parent=SQUASH_B
TREE_H2=$(git rev-parse 222d0c21^{tree}) && \
H2=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Test User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:06 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Test User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:06 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_H2 -p $SQUASH_B -m "feat: add process function") && \
echo "H2=$H2" && \

# Squash C: 3 bot commits (074f9292, 59262c1a, c961c7b5) after H2
TREE_C=$(git rev-parse c961c7b5^{tree}) && \
SQUASH_C=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Bot User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:07 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Bot User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="bot@company.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:07 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_C -p $H2 -m "chore: [bot] squashed 3 automated commits") && \
echo "SQUASH_C=$SQUASH_C" && \

# Human commit 3: feat: add cleanup function (d8ae9b40), parent=SQUASH_C
TREE_H3=$(git rev-parse d8ae9b40^{tree}) && \
H3=$(GIT_AUTHOR_NAME="Test User" GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_AUTHOR_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:08 +0000" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_NAME="Test User" GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL="test@example.com" \
  GIT_COMMITTER_DATE="2026-03-11 12:33:08 +0000" \
  git commit-tree $TREE_H3 -p $SQUASH_C -m "feat: add cleanup function") && \
echo "H3=$H3" && \

# Store all SHAs for later use
echo "ROOT=$ROOT SQUASH_A=$SQUASH_A H1=$H1 SQUASH_B=$SQUASH_B H2=$H2 SQUASH_C=$SQUASH_C H3=$H3" > /tmp/new_shas.txt && cat /tmp/new_shas.txt

parts.text: New commits created. Now I'll verify the chain is correct, then import into jj and move the `main` bookmark.

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && git log --oneline 67c500d7091154dd1f4246cb56ccd4fba25e186e

output.output:
67c500d feat: add cleanup function
312165f chore: [bot] squashed 3 automated commits
ed5ff03 feat: add process function
74eab2a chore: [bot] squashed 2 automated commits
059ca1b feat: add main module
9c37dd4 chore: [bot] squashed 3 automated commits
3736232 feat: initial data setup

parts.text: The new chain looks perfect — 7 commits instead of 12, with squashed bot groups. Now move the `main` bookmark in jj:

input.command:
cd /home/user/repo && \
NEW_TIP=67c500d7091154dd1f4246cb56ccd4fba25e186e && \
# Import the new git commits into jj
jj git import 2>&1 && \
echo "--- import done ---" && \
# Move main bookmark to new tip
jj bookmark set main --to $NEW_TIP 2>&1 && \
echo "--- bookmark set ---" && \
jj bookmark list 2>&1

input.command:
cd /home/user/repo && \
# jj uses the .git directory - our commits already exist there
# Check git refs
git log --oneline 67c500d7 2>/dev/null && \
echo "---" && \
# jj git import should pick them up - let's force via git ref creation first
git update-ref refs/heads/new_main 67c500d7091154dd1f4246cb56ccd4fba25e186e && \
jj git import 2>&1 && \
echo "--- after import ---" && \
jj log --no-graph --template 'commit_id.short() ++ " " ++ author.email() ++ " " ++ description.first_line() ++ "\n"' 2>/dev/null | head -20

parts.text: The new commits are imported. Now set the `main` bookmark to the new tip:

input.command:
cd /home/user/repo && \
NEW_TIP=67c500d7091154dd1f4246cb56ccd4fba25e186e && \
# Set main bookmark in jj using the new commit
jj bookmark set main --to 67c500d70911 2>&1 && \
echo "--- done ---" && \
jj bookmark list 2>&1

input.command:
cd /home/user/repo && \
# The working copy commit (d8ae9b40) is currently where main points
# We need to use --allow-backwards since jj sees the new tip as unrelated/parallel
jj bookmark set main --to 67c500d70911 --allow-backwards 2>&1 && \
echo "--- done ---" && \
jj bookmark list 2>&1
